headline: Cross-origin `/health` regression test and `CORS_ORIGINS` documentation
summary: Adds a security-suite test that locks in the v1.11.14 `/health` CORS contract — same-origin probes pass, listed gateway origins receive a matching `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, unlisted origins are refused. Also documents `CORS_ORIGINS` in `.env.example` and clarifies in the README that browser-driven probes from a separate gateway origin need that origin on the allowlist.

items.title: `tests/security/cors-health.test.js`
items.body: New security-suite test boots a minimal `b.router.Router` wired with `b.middleware.cors` using the same options `middleware/cors.js` passes (`refuseUnknown: true`, credentials enabled, single configured gateway origin), mounts the `/health` handler verbatim from `server-main.js`, and asserts three cases — same-origin (no `Origin` header) returns 200, listed origin returns 200 with matching `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, unlisted origin returns a non-2xx with no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`. Closes the coverage gap that allowed the v1.11.13 CORS regression to ship.

items.title: `.env.example` documents `CORS_ORIGINS`
items.body: Adds a `CORS_ORIGINS=` stanza alongside `TRUST_PROXY`, explaining that this is the single allowlist that governs every cross-origin browser request (including `/health` probes from a separate gateway origin) and that wildcards are refused when credentials are enabled. Also tightens the `TRUST_PROXY` comment to mention the single-trusted-hop form (`127.0.0.1`).

items.title: README clarifies cross-origin `/health` requirements
items.body: The Health check section now states explicitly that same-origin probes (container `HEALTHCHECK`, in-pod liveness, TLS-terminating proxy) need no extra config, while a browser-driven probe from a different origin (the static PQC entry page) requires that origin on `CORS_ORIGINS` — listing the symptom (`403` + browser rejection) so operators can diagnose the misconfiguration without reading the source.
